title: Quiz: Quantum Trading Strategies
description: Test your understanding of quantum-enhanced trading strategies

questions.question: What is market regime classification used for in trading?

options.text: To identify market conditions and adapt strategy accordingly

questions.explanation: Market regime classification identifies whether the market is trending, mean-reverting, volatile, or calm. Different strategies work better in different regimes, so adapting to the current regime improves performance.

questions.question: What is QAOA primarily used for in quantum trading?

options.text: Solving optimization problems like portfolio allocation

questions.explanation: QAOA (Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm) is designed to solve combinatorial optimization problems. In trading, it's used for portfolio optimization, finding optimal allocations among discrete choices.

questions.question: In a trending market regime, what trading approach typically works best?

options.text: Fade all moves (bet against the trend)

questions.explanation: In trending markets, trend-following strategies work best. The approach is to identify the trend direction and trade in that direction until the regime changes.

questions.question: What does a mean-reverting regime suggest?

options.text: Prices will continue in one direction
Price moves tend to reverse and return to average

questions.explanation: In a mean-reverting regime, prices tend to bounce back after moving away from their average. Strategies that fade recent moves (sell after rises, buy after falls) work well in this regime.

questions.question: Quantum feature maps encode classical data into quantum states for processing.
questions.explanation: True. Quantum feature maps transform classical features (like price returns, volatility) into quantum states through rotation gates. This allows quantum circuits to process the data and potentially find patterns not easily visible classically.

questions.question: Why is momentum consistency important in quantum momentum strategies?

options.text: Signals are stronger when multiple timeframes agree

questions.explanation: Momentum consistency measures whether multiple lookback periods show the same direction. When all timeframes (5-day, 10-day, 20-day) agree on direction, the signal is more reliable and has higher confidence.

questions.question: What is a key challenge in QAOA portfolio optimization?

options.text: The algorithm only works with two assets
The solution space must be discretized

questions.explanation: QAOA works with discrete optimization problems, so continuous portfolio weights must be discretized (e.g., 0%, 10%, 20%...). This introduces approximation but makes the problem suitable for quantum optimization.

questions.question: Which are advantages of quantum pattern recognition in trading? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Quantum pattern recognition can identify complex non-linear patterns, may offer speedups for high-dimensional data, and operates in quantum feature spaces. However, it cannot eliminate all trading losses - markets remain inherently uncertain.
